decisions.reasoning: NVDA reported transformative Q1 FY2027 earnings on May 20: revenue $81.6B (+85% YoY), beat $78B guidance; EPS $1.87 vs $1.78 expected; Q2 guide $91B (+95% YoY, vs $87.2B consensus); data center revenue $75.2B (+92% YoY). Board approved 2,400% dividend increase and $80B buyback. Post-earnings dip driven by supply constraint concerns (memory/DRAM/powered-shell availability) viewed as noise by Baird (raised target to $500), Gene Munster, Jim Cramer. SMA signal long. Added 10 shares at ~$216.79 to existing 9-share position, bringing total to 19.

decisions.invalidation: Supply constraints materially worse than guided; geopolitical export restrictions on H100/Blackwell; competitor AI chip winning significant datacenter share

decisions.reasoning: Q3 beat with revenue +15% YoY to $82.9B, but SMA signal is flat (fast 415.72 ≈ slow 415.99), FCF compressed at $15.8B from $190B capex plan (+61% YoY), sophisticated hedge funds TCI and Rokos have exited. Holding as fundamentals intact and consensus target $587 implies long-term upside; not selling into a small loss without new downside catalyst.

decisions.reasoning: Broad market uptrend intact — up 8.1% YTD, SMA signal long. 78% of S&P 500 companies beat Q1 estimates. Yardeni raised year-end target to 8,250. Risks: sticky inflation (Goldman pushes Fed cuts to Dec 2026/Mar 2027), elevated valuations. Hold as portfolio diversifier.

decisions.reasoning: Bought yesterday (May 21). WTI near $90 driven by Iran conflict supply shock. XLE up 21.6% in 2026. UAE left OPEC on May 1. Bear case is real: IEA projected 3.2M bbl/day surplus before conflict, and a ceasefire would trigger sharp oil retrace. Too new (1 day) to reverse without direct de-escalation catalyst.

summary: First session: added 10 shares NVDA on post-earnings dip after transformative Q1 FY2027 beat; held MSFT, SPY, XLE
open_theses: NVDA: AI datacenter capex cycle intact, Q2 guide $91B sets high floor; supply constraint dip is buyable
XLE: Iran-conflict geopolitical premium holding oil near $90; watch for ceasefire signals as exit trigger
MSFT: Flat SMA momentum from capex concerns; hold for AI monetization evidence in Q4 FY2026
SPY: Broad market uptrend, hold as ballast; reassess if Goldman delayed Fed cut thesis gains traction
